danger_signals.visual_form: Player vehicle sent back to track after leaving defined route
danger_signals.meaning: Player has strayed from safe racing path and must reposition

danger_signals.visual_form: Cereal boxes on breakfast tables, pencil sharpeners on desks, environmental hazards
danger_signals.meaning: Physical dangers embedded in track environment that impede progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent vehicle visible on-screen in same view
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat from competing racer; losing positional advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear racing route marked through household environments
safety_signals.meaning: Established safe corridor for vehicle movement

safety_signals.visual_form: First or second place finish in race round
safety_signals.meaning: Player has survived gauntlet and earned progression to next challenge

safety_signals.visual_form: Time trial completion every three races grants additional chance
safety_signals.meaning: Safety net extended; player gains buffer against elimination

faction_indicators.color_identity: One of two player colors (color-coded in head-to-head mode)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Light indicator on screen matching player color

faction_indicators.color_identity: Opposite color from Player 1 in competitive modes
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Light indicator on screen matching player color
AI-controlled vehicles in Challenge mode (three opponents per race)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct vehicle sprites from player-controlled cars
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Guided by invisible arrow system on track

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three computer opponents visible on single screen racing simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI vehicles follow predetermined invisible arrow paths; player must finish 1st or 2nd to progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two vehicles on shared single screen; light indicators (eight total) at screen edge show progress
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gaining screen distance converts lights to their color; leader becomes invisible when distance sufficient

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Both players observe same single-screen view; color-coded vehicles with potential handicap modifier on one
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Direct head-to-head racing with symmetrical ruleset; handicap applied to balance skill differences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Solo racing against clock; timer displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Completion grants extra life; extends player survival in Challenge mode
Player can no longer progress through Challenge rounds; final failure triggers game end state

environmental_storytelling.example: Racing on breakfast tables with cereal box obstacles, pool tables, work desks with pencil sharpeners, treehouses — environments communicate the 'micro' scale of toy vehicles through recognizable oversized household props
Formula One cars, powerboats, helicopters, and tanks as selectable vehicles convey different thematic racing scenarios within same miniature world contexts
Cereal boxes and pencil sharpeners are not generic barriers but specific environmental hazards that anchor races in authentic domestic settings
Forcing players back to predefined path when leaving route communicates the bounded 'tabletop' nature of the racing arena — tracks are finite household surface spaces

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responsive to player input; maintains position in single shared view
Automatically repositioned back to track; brief penalty applied
Follows invisible arrow navigation system; maintains consistent difficulty through AI pathfinding
Eliminated from current race; replaced in next race round if player qualifies
Starting position or vehicle stat modifier applied to balance against stronger opponent
Becomes invisible when sufficient lead gained; opponent sees only empty track ahead

notes: Micro Machines NES employs a minimalist visual communication system appropriate to 8-bit hardware. World-state is conveyed primarily through: (1) light indicator progression in head-to-head mode, (2) screen position relativity between players, (3) environmental theming (household environments communicate scale narrative), (4) single-screen constraint that forces proximity-based competition mechanics. The game lacks faction complexity or political state — competition is purely mechanical and positional. Danger and safety signals are embedded in track design (boundaries, obstacles) and game-rule progression (qualification/elimination). The 'world state' is essentially the game mode active and round progression; there is no persistent world narrative or environmental storytelling beyond the toy-scale household settings.
